notes: A long agentic turn carrying MANY sequential tool calls in ONE turn, validating that state tracking stays coherent across the burst: the session stays continuously working (no flicker to ready or waiting between successive AssistantMessage(toolUse)/ToolResults pairs), the turn settles cleanly to ready, and the session is never stuck. kiro-cli launched with --trust-all-tools has NO permission gate (it emits no AskUser/permission event), so it runs the real many-tool-call single-turn shape natively — like the committed codex cell, and unlike the claudecode recipe which must fall back to 4 plain prompts. The kiro-cli parser maps AssistantMessage(toolUse) -> assistant (stays working), ToolResults -> tool_result (closes the open call), and ONLY a text-only AssistantMessage -> turn_done (the single ready settle); Prompt -> user_message (working). So the daemon observes: proc-PID pre-session born ready, the rollout session reconcile (new session_id, kiro mints its own UUID) born ready, ONE ready->working at the prompt, ONE contiguous working span across the whole burst, ONE working->ready at the final text-only AssistantMessage. The burst_working dwell (duration_at_least_ms 4000) with no-ready/no-waiting invariants is the load-bearing no-flicker assertion: if any intermediate tool result flickered the session to ready or waiting mid-burst, this phase would fire. The DSL has no command-count form, so the minimum-tool-call assertion lives in the recipe verify and the transcript's AssistantMessage(toolUse)/ToolResults pairs, not as a phase. teardown is omitted: kiro-cli process exit DELETES the session by design (final observed state is ready while alive). observations assert kiro-cli as the agent only: kiro's JSONL carries NO model/token/cost fields (those live in the <uuid>.json sidecar the v1 adapter does not read), so model/cost/tokens are deliberately NOT asserted nonzero — they would be empty/zero and fail. Exact command count, tool output text, and token totals vary run-to-run (model/OS-dependent) and are NOT asserted.

text: Pre-session (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 2s of kiro-cli launch
Rollout session reconciles (kiro mints its own UUID, different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session via cwd match (kiro-cli parent process)
User prompt -> working on the rollout session (start of the agentic burst)
State stays continuously working across the many sequential tool calls — no flicker to ready or waiting between successive AssistantMessage(toolUse)/ToolResults pairs in the same turn
After the final tool call and summary the turn completes (text-only AssistantMessage) -> back to ready; state never entered waiting during the burst; final state ready
